llm_response:
# Resignation Letter

Dear [Boss's Name],

I am writing to formally inform you of my decision to resign from my position as [Your Job Title], with my last day of employment set for [Date]. This was not an easy decision to make, as I have truly valued my time here, but I have come to the conclusion that it is the right step for me to take at this time. I want to ensure that this process is handled smoothly and respectfully, and I am committed to working with you to finalize the details of my resignation before my departure.

After careful consideration, I have identified several factors that have led me to this point, primarily focusing on my long-term career trajectory and current personal circumstances. Specifically, I am seeking new challenges that align more closely with my professional goals, and I feel that my current role no longer offers the necessary opportunities for the growth I desire. Additionally, certain personal matters require my full attention, which makes it difficult for me to dedicate the same level of energy and focus to my work as I have in the past. While I am grateful for the support I have received, I believe that stepping away now will allow me to address these areas effectively.

I want to express my sincere gratitude for the opportunity to work under your leadership and for the opportunity to be part of such a dedicated team. To ensure a seamless transition, I have outlined the following key points for our discussion:
*   I will complete all pending projects before my departure date to minimize disruption.
*   I am willing to train a replacement or document my current workflows in detail.
*   I will return all company property and access credentials by my final day.

Thank you again for everything, and I hope we can maintain a positive relationship moving forward.
